sections.paragraph:
inf. יְרֹה, יְרֹות, and יְרֹוא 2Ch 26:15, imp. יְרֵה, fut. יִירֶה [1 pers. pl. with suff. נִירָם].

(1) to cast, Exo 15:4 e.g. a lot, Jos 18:6 an arrow, 1Sa 20:36, 37 1Sa 20:37 Psa 11:2 64:5 Num 21:30. Part. יֹורֶה, an archer, 1Ch 10:3.

(2) to lay foundations (compare Gr. Βάλλεσθαι ἄστυ, for to lay the foundations of a city; Syr. ܪܡܳܐ to cast, to place a foundation), to found. Job 38:6, “who laid (or founded, or placed) its corner stone.” Gen 31:51, “behold the pillar אֲשֶׁר יָרִיתִי which I have founded,” i.e. placed, or raised.

(3) to sprinkle, to water (pr. to throw water, to scatter drops, compare זָרַק), Hos 6:3. Hence part. יֹורֶה the former rain, see above, page 343, B .

Niphal, pass. of No. 1, to be shot through with darts or arrows. Fut. יִיָּרֶה Exo 19:13.

Hiphil הֹורָח, fut. יֹורֶה, with Vav convers. וַיֹּור.

(1) i.q. Kal, to cast, Job 30:19 specially arrows, 1Sa 20:20, 36 1Sa 20:36. Fut. apoc. וַיֹּור 2Ki 13:17. Part. מֹורֶה an archer, 1Sa 31:3. Written by an Aramæism in the manner of verbs לא֞, 2Sa 11:24, וַיֹּרְאוּ הַמֹּורְאִים “and the archers shot,” comp. 2Ch 26:15.

(2) to sprinkle, to water. Part. מֹורֶה i.q. יֹורֶה “the former rain,” Joe 2:23 Psa 84:7.

(3) to send out the hand (like שָׁלַח יָד), especially for pointing out. Hence to show, to indicate, Gen 46:28. Pro 6:13, מֹרֶה בְּאֶצְבְּעֹתָיו “showing (i.e. giving signals) with his fingers;” followed by two accusatives, of the person and of the thing, Exo 15:26.

From pointing out or shewing it is-(4) to teach, to instruct (comp. Gr. δεικνύω, ἀναφαίνω), Exo 35:34 Mic 3:11 followed by an acc. of pers. to instruct any one, Job 6:24 8:10 12:7 , Job 12:8followed by an acc. of the thing, Isa 9:14 Hab 2:18. Hence followed by two accusatives, of the person and of the thing, Psa 27:11 86:11 119:33 followed by בְּ of the thing (properly to instruct in something), Job 27:11 Psa 25:8, 12 Psa 25:12 32:8 more rarely followed by אֶל (prop. to teach or conform to something) 2Ch 6:27 or מִן (to instruct concerning) Isa 2:3 Mic 4:2. Followed by a dative of pers. and acc. of the thing, Deu 33:10 Hos 10:12.

Derived nouns, יֹורֶה, מֹורֶה, תֹּורָה [also יֹורָה, יֹורַי, יְרוּאֵל, יְרוּשָׁלַיִם, יְרִיאֵל, יְרִיָּה].
